integrations_researched.name: Anthropic SDK TypeScript

api_patterns.initialization: const client = new Anthropic({ apiKey: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY });
api_patterns.structured_output: Use system prompts to request JSON output, then parse the response text content

gotchas: Response content is in blocks array, need to extract text from 'text' type blocks
System prompts are separate parameter, not in messages array
For structured JSON output, must parse response text manually (no native structured output API)

integrations_researched.name: OpenAI SDK

api_patterns.initialization: const client = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY });
api_patterns.structured_output: Use client.chat.completions.parse() with zodResponseFormat() for type-safe structured JSON responses

gotchas: Structured outputs require gpt-4o-2024-08-06 or later models
Must use zodResponseFormat() helper to convert Zod schemas to JSON Schema format
Access parsed data via message.parsed property when using .parse() method
LengthFinishReasonError thrown if response truncated, ContentFilterFinishReasonError if blocked

integrations_researched.name: Drizzle ORM

imports: import { eq, and, or, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
import { pgTable, text, timestamp, jsonb, pgEnum } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';

key_fields: category (enum: technical_seo, content_optimization, schema_markup, citation_building, brand_consistency, competitor_analysis, content_freshness, authority_building)

gotchas: Use .$dynamic() to enable multiple where() calls or conditional query building
Use .toSQL() to inspect generated SQL queries for debugging
JSONB fields need type annotations: jsonb('field').$type<Type>()
Relations must be defined separately using relations() function
Enums must be defined with pgEnum() before using in schema

integrations_researched.name: Pinecone Vector Database

api_patterns.initialization: const pc = new Pinecone({ apiKey: process.env.PINECONE_API_KEY });

gotchas: Index must be created before use (check if exists first)
Vectors must match the dimension specified when index was created
Metadata is optional but highly recommended for filtering
Use namespaces to organize data within a single index
Query results include similarity scores for ranking

integrations_researched.name: Next.js

api_patterns.api_routes: app/api/[route]/route.ts with named exports: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE

gotchas: App Router uses app/ directory, not pages/
Server Components are default, client components need 'use client'
Environment variables must be prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ for client access
API routes must be in app/api/ with route.ts files

integrations_researched.name: Zod

api_patterns.verified_against: Package documentation and OpenAI integration examples

gotchas: parse() throws on validation failure, safeParse() returns result object
Use z.infer<typeof Schema> to get TypeScript type from schema
Combine with zodResponseFormat() for OpenAI structured outputs
research_sources: OpenAI SDK documentation for structured outputs

integrations_researched.name: Neon PostgreSQL

api_patterns.initialization: const pool = new Pool({ connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL });

gotchas: Serverless-optimized, works with edge runtimes
Connection pooling managed by Neon
DATABASE_URL format: postgresql://user:password@host/database

recommendations.recommendation: Use OpenAI structured outputs (client.chat.completions.parse with Zod schemas) for generating recommendations
recommendations.rationale: OpenAI provides native structured output support with type-safe parsing, eliminating manual JSON parsing errors. The zodResponseFormat() helper ensures responses conform to defined schemas.
recommendations.implementation: Define Zod schema for recommendations with fields: title, description, category, priority, impact, effort, steps[], reasoning. Use gpt-4o model with response_format parameter.

recommendations.recommendation: Leverage existing recommendations table schema - it already has all required fields
recommendations.rationale: The recommendations table in src/lib/db/schema/recommendations.ts already includes priority, impact, effort, status, steps, and source fields needed for AI-generated recommendations.
recommendations.implementation: No schema changes needed. AI service will populate: title, description, category, priority, impact, effort, estimatedTime, steps, source='monitoring'

recommendations.recommendation: Use Pinecone to retrieve relevant brand visibility data before generating recommendations
recommendations.rationale: Vector similarity search can identify related brand mentions, content gaps, and patterns across platforms to provide context for AI recommendation generation.
recommendations.implementation: Query Pinecone with brand embeddings to find similar mentions, low-performing content, or competitor data. Pass top results as context to LLM.

recommendations.recommendation: Implement multi-factor scoring algorithm combining AI prediction with historical data
recommendations.rationale: Pure AI predictions may not align with actual business metrics. Combine AI-suggested priority with factors like: visibility gap size, competitor performance, platform importance, historical ROI.

recommendations.recommendation: Create dedicated API route: POST /api/recommendations/generate with brandId parameter
recommendations.rationale: Separates recommendation generation from other APIs, allows async processing for long-running AI analysis.
recommendations.implementation: app/api/recommendations/generate/route.ts - accepts brandId, retrieves brand data, calls AI service, saves to database, returns recommendations

recommendations.recommendation: Implement fallback to Claude if OpenAI structured outputs fail
recommendations.rationale: Both providers are already configured. Claude can serve as backup if OpenAI rate limits hit or structured output parsing fails.
recommendations.implementation: Use existing LLM router (src/lib/ai/router.ts) to automatically failover between providers

recommendations.recommendation: Cache generated recommendations for 24 hours using existing Upstash Redis
recommendations.rationale: Recommendation generation is expensive. Cache results to avoid redundant AI calls for same brand data.
recommendations.implementation: Key format: 'recommendations:{brandId}:{dataHash}' - invalidate on new brand visibility data

recommendations.recommendation: Use existing Vitest setup to test recommendation generation logic
recommendations.rationale: Project already has Vitest configured. Create unit tests for scoring algorithm, integration tests for AI service.

data_flow: 2. Retrieve brand data from brands table (monitoring platforms, competitors, keywords)
3. Query Pinecone for recent brand mentions/visibility data
4. Aggregate visibility metrics (citation count, sentiment, platform coverage)
5. Identify gaps (missing platforms, negative sentiment areas, competitor advantages)
6. Format context for LLM prompt with brand info + visibility data + gaps
7. Call OpenAI with structured output schema for recommendations array
8. Parse AI response, calculate impact scores using scoring algorithm
9. Insert recommendations into database with source='monitoring'
prompt_engineering: Include brand voice and key messages in system prompt
Provide competitor data for competitive analysis recommendations
Specify monitoring platforms to tailor platform-specific recommendations
Request actionable steps in imperative voice (Do X, Create Y, Update Z)
Ask for estimated effort/time based on recommendation complexity
Include content gap analysis in prompt context
performance_considerations: Batch process recommendations for multiple brands asynchronously
Use gpt-4o-mini for faster, cheaper initial screening
Use gpt-4o for final recommendation generation and prioritization
Implement rate limiting to avoid API quota exhaustion
Stream responses for better UX on long-running requests
